_status: AWAITING SIGNOFF 2026-07-12 — James River assembled from verified research (james-findings-partial.md + verified-identifiers-james.md written this pass). Two [verify]-cleared gauges (07052500 Galena, 07050700 Springfield). Lower/Galena reach is calibrated: optimal_min/optimal_max from moherp OBSERVED 'Good' ratings, high/dangerous from James River Outfitters 7 ft / 8 ft gage-height limits converted to cfs via the USGS 07052500 exsa rating (unit decision RESOLVED: whole Galena ladder in cfs, one unit per gauge). UPPER/URBAN REACH (Hwy 125 -> Hootentown) IS UNCALIBRATED BY DESIGN: no OBSERVED floatability source exists, so its thresholds[] are intentionally EMPTY and it must NOT drive a live badge (insufficient-data). ACCESS GPS PENDING: only Shelvin Rock, Hootentown, and the Galena Y-Bridge carry coordinates; Delaware Town and Kissick Dam coords omitted, all river miles unconfirmed — a human places every access point in admin. Owner reviews thresholds, the ft-vs-cfs decision, and the cabins conflict at James River Outfitters, then flips _status to begin with SIGNED-OFF.
name: James River
description: A southwest-Missouri float and smallmouth stream draining the Springfield plateau to Table Rock Lake. The Galena reach is the classic float; upper reaches near Springfield carry urban runoff.
managingAuthority: Missouri Department of Conservation (state-managed accesses); streambed navigability per Missouri law

accessLaw.value: Missouri follows the "navigable in fact" doctrine: the public may float and wade streams navigable in fact, but streambeds/banks are frequently privately owned, so access is legal from public road crossings, public land, and established accesses, while portaging or camping on private gravel bars can be trespass. Navigability is decided stream by stream; there is no statewide list. (Shared Missouri paragraph — confirm the AGO/MDC citation once for the state.)
accessLaw.source: https://mdc.mo.gov/ (MDC stream access) — confirm AGO citation once for MO

sections.name: Lower / Galena reach (Hootentown → Galena, ~22 mi)
sections.description: The classic lower-James float: Hootentown down to the Galena Y-Bridge, ~22 river miles of clear, spring-influenced Class I water through the Springfield Plateau of the Ozarks. Gravel bars, occasional bluffs, and a noted smallmouth-bass fishery (a Smallmouth Bass Special Management Area begins near Hootentown). This is the recreational reach served by the Galena outfitters.

representativeGauge.rationale: James River at Galena (07052500) is the long-record USGS gauge at the Galena Y-Bridge take-out, co-located with the reach's downstream end, carries both discharge (00060) and gage height (00065), and is the datum both the moherp community ratings and James River Outfitters quote against — so every Galena anchor references a gauge we actually poll.
representativeGauge.knownBias: sits at the downstream (take-out) end of the ~22 mi reach; the Hootentown put-in ~22 mi upstream runs somewhat lower, so a near-minimum reading here means the top of the reach is already thin.

thresholds.source: rivers.moherp.org OBSERVED 'Good' community breakpoint for James at Galena 07052500 (https://rivers.moherp.org/gauge/?gauge=07052500)
inferred from moherp OBSERVED 'Good' trip reports up to 937 cfs
James River Outfitters caution level = 7 ft gage height, converted to cfs via the USGS 07052500 exsa rating (7.00 ft = 3079 cfs)

James River Outfitters closes the river above 8 ft gage height ('no boats, no exceptions'), converted to cfs via the USGS 07052500 exsa rating (8.00 ft = 4410 cfs). Deliberately NOT the NWS flood stage (15 ft).

publishedFloatTimes.source: james-findings-partial.md — full lower-James run Hootentown→Galena = 22 mi (the classic reach served by the Galena outfitters); per-vessel float hours not captured (toVerify for speed calibration).

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: below ~180 cfs boats drag on riffles
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: outfitter closes above 8 ft gage height

hazards.value: Strainers (downed trees and logjams on outside bends) are the main in-water hazard on this Class I gravel-bed reach.
hazards.source: james-findings-partial.md (lower James reach character — primary hazards strainer/rapid)

hazards.value: Rising water: James River Outfitters posts a caution zone from 7 ft and a do-not-float limit at 8 ft gage height on USGS 07052500 ('no boats, no exceptions').
hazards.source: James River Outfitters water-levels page (gage-height limits converted to cfs via the USGS 07052500 exsa rating)

localKnowledge: The James is a noted smallmouth-bass fishery: a Smallmouth Bass Special Management Area (SMA) begins near the Hootentown access at the upstream end of this reach and runs downstream — special length/creel regulations apply, so confirm current MDC SMA rules before keeping fish.

Two Galena outfitters serve the lower reach: James River Outfitters (canoe/kayak/raft/SUP/tube rental, shuttle, camping/RV, store) and Float the James / Horsecreek Ranch (tube/kayak/SUP, shuttle, camping, cabins).
The full Hootentown → Galena Y-Bridge run is ~22 mi — the classic lower-James float; shorter days are made by putting in or taking out at intermediate accesses.

sections.name: Upper / urban reach (Hwy 125 → Hootentown)
sections.description: The upper James from around Highway 125 down to Hootentown, draining Springfield and its urban fringe. Flashier and more rain-driven than the spring-fed lower reach, with urban runoff and Springfield wastewater-treatment-plant influence on water quality. Floats only at medium/high water and is not the recreational reach. Carries a low-head dam hazard (Kissick Dam).

representativeGauge.rationale: James River near Springfield (07050700) is the only USGS gauge on the upper/urban reach, but it is UNCALIBRATED — no OBSERVED floatability source exists for this reach, so it carries no thresholds and must not drive a live condition badge (insufficient-data).
representativeGauge.knownBias: urban headwater gauge; flashy Springfield runoff and WWTP influence; reads differently from the spring-fed lower reach. Drainage area and NWS LID unknown (toVerify).

characteristics.risingWaterHazards: flashy urban runoff; Springfield WWTP water-quality

hazards.value: Kissick Dam (low-head dam) at approximately river mile 12.3 — a drowning-machine hazard; portage or scout, do NOT run it.
hazards.source: james-findings-partial.md — Kissick Dam ~mi 12.3, low-head dam HAZARD (portage/scout)

hazards.value: Urban runoff and Springfield wastewater-treatment-plant (WWTP) discharge affect water quality on this reach, which is flashier than the spring-fed lower reach.

localKnowledge: This reach carries Springfield urban runoff and is rain-flashy; it floats only at medium/high water and is not the recreational reach — most floaters use the lower Hootentown → Galena run.
No OBSERVED floatability calibration exists for the upper reach; only scattered trip reports. It is intentionally left uncalibrated (thresholds empty) until a floater-sourced anchor is found.

accessPoints.name: Shelvin Rock Access
accessPoints.notes: MDC access on the upper James, upstream of Hootentown. Coordinates verified; river mile and facility details pending human placement.

accessPoints.name: Hootentown Access
accessPoints.notes: MDC access marking the upstream end of the smallmouth Special Management Area (SMA) and the boundary between the upper/urban reach and the lower/Galena float reach — the put-in for the classic ~22 mi Hootentown→Galena float. Coordinates verified; river mile pending.
accessPoints.source: james-findings-partial.md (access-point coordinates verified; SMA upstream end)

accessPoints.name: Galena Y-Bridge Access
accessPoints.notes: Standard take-out for the lower James at the Galena Y-Bridge, co-located with the primary gauge USGS 07052500 (James River at Galena). Coordinates verified; ownership (city/MDC) and river mile pending verification.
accessPoints.source: james-findings-partial.md (take-out at the gauge; coordinates verified)

accessPoints.name: Delaware Town Access
accessPoints.notes: Listed James River access; coordinates, ownership, and river mile UNKNOWN (toVerify) — human places the point in admin. One of several accesses (with HL Kerr and others) not yet located.

accessPoints.name: Kissick Dam
accessPoints.notes: Low-head dam on the UPPER reach at approximately river mile 12.3 — a drowning-machine hazard; portage or scout, do NOT run. Not a launch point; recorded as a mapped hazard. Coordinates omitted (toVerify).

sourcesConsulted: James River Outfitters water-levels page (jamesriveroutfitters.com) — 7 ft caution / 8 ft do-not-float
USGS 07052500 exsa rating curve (waterdata.usgs.gov get_ratings, exsa)
openQuestions: UPPER/URBAN REACH DANGER IS UNKNOWN BY DESIGN: the Hwy 125 → Hootentown reach has NO OBSERVED floatability calibration — no moherp OBSERVED rating and no outfitter do-not-float number exist for it (the ESTIMATED tier is rejected per the calibration hierarchy). Its thresholds[] are intentionally EMPTY and representativeGauge 07050700 is uncalibrated. This reach must NOT drive a live condition badge; it reads insufficient-data until a floater-sourced anchor is captured.

GALENA <180 cfs CURRENTLY READS 'too_low': no too_low/low anchor was sourced (moherp gives only an OBSERVED 'Good' breakpoint at 180 cfs; the ESTIMATED tier is rejected), so the too_low anchor is intentionally OMITTED. Condition logic reads [—, optimal_min=180) as 'too_low', which is acceptable for now, but a genuine low/too_low anchor (an Agnew navigability table or an outfitter minimum) should be captured so the badge can distinguish 'low but floatable' from 'too low to float'.

GALENA UNIT DECISION (resolved, confirm at signoff): all four Galena anchors are encoded in cfs — the outfitter ft limits (7 ft caution / 8 ft do-not-float) were converted to cfs via the USGS 07052500 exsa rating so the whole ladder shares one unit (the [auto] one-unit-per-gauge gate requires it). The findings flagged an owner choice of badging in ft vs cfs; cfs was chosen to match the moherp optimal band. Owner to confirm.

NHD Permanent_Identifier GUID is UNKNOWN — nhdFeatureId left 'UNKNOWN'. We DO have GNIS 750497, NHD reachcode 11010002000005, and NHDPlus COMID 7609749; resolve the flowline's Permanent_Identifier GUID on the National Map NHDPlus_HR (MO HUC 11010002) to fill nhdFeatureId.

SPRINGFIELD GAUGE 07050700: drainage area (sq mi) and NWS LID are UNKNOWN — needed before the upper reach can be drainage-area scaled or flood-cross-checked. (Galena 07052500 LID = GLNM7, action 11 ft / flood 15 ft, CROSS-CHECK ONLY, not the anchor.)

ACCESS-POINT GPS PENDING: only Shelvin Rock, Hootentown, and the Galena Y-Bridge carry coordinates; Delaware Town, HL Kerr, and other James accesses need coordinates + ownership + river miles (a human places every point in admin). All river miles are unconfirmed.

CABINS CONFLICT AT JAMES RIVER OUTFITTERS: the findings list James River Outfitters as outfitter + campground (no cabins) but Float the James / Horsecreek Ranch as offering cabins — confirm whether James River Outfitters itself offers cabins before publishing lodging info.

MIDDLE-GAUGE CANDIDATE (not adopted): 07052250 James River near Boaz (37.00658, -93.36467, drainage 462, 00060+00065, LID JAMM7) sits between the two reaches; the two-reach model keys Galena to 07052500 and the upper reach to 07050700, so Boaz is not in gauges[]. Revisit if a middle reach is later split out.
